title: vinca minor var. azurea
intro: Vinca minor var. azurea, also known as the periwinkle or blue periwinkle, is a flowering plant belonging to the Apocynaceae family, characterized by its blue-purple flowers and blue-green, evergreen foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of diabetes, as it contains the alkaloids vincamine and ajmalicine, which have been found to have hypoglycemic and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Vinca minor var. azurea include its potential to reduce blood sugar levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of capsules, tablets, or teas, and can also be used topically to treat wounds and skin conditions.
